message:
feat: Implement proper Python format spec parsing for f-strings

- Add FormatSpec struct and parseFormatSpec() to parse Python's format
  mini-language: [[fill]align][sign][#][0][width][,][.precision][type]
- Handle alternate form (#), zero-padding (0), alignment (<>^=), sign
  (+- ), width, precision, and type specifiers (x, X, o, b, d, s, etc.)
- Fix zero-padding to be sign-aware (sign/prefix before zeros)
- Use runtime.pyFormat for all f-string format specs instead of trying
  to convert Python format specs to Zig format specs

Example: f"{255:#08x}" now correctly outputs "0x0000ff"
